reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): The code check at tasks.rs:42 validates total column count (available_columns.len() * 2 < P::NumberOfColumns::USIZE) but does NOT validate per-blob cell availability. The recover_matrix function (eip_7594/src/lib.rs:228-250) groups entries by row_index (blob) and calls recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs for each blob independently, but there is no check that each blob has ≥50% cells before recovery. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can cause this via P2P by selectively providing columns with uneven blob distribution. If 64/128 columns are available but unevenly distributed (e.g., columns 0-63 each contain cell for blob A but only columns 0-31 contain cell for blob B), the global check passes but blob B has only 25% cells. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-57888860-pre-003 (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json:132-147) requires 'forall recover(cell_indices, cells): len(cell_indices) >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2'. The code violates this by checking aggregate column count instead of per-blob cell count. Severity: Downgraded from HIGH to Medium because the spec-compliant KZG library (recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs_raw) will likely return an error when given <50% cells, causing reconstruction to fail gracefully (tasks.rs:75-82) rather than producing invalid data. The impact is DoS (reconstruction failure) rather than data corruption. However, the error path doesn't distinguish this specific failure mode, and the theoretical risk of incorrect recovery exists if the underlying library doesn't validate the threshold.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Recovery requires at least CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB/2 cells (50% erasure coding threshold). Assertion: forall recover(cell_indices, cells): len(cell_indices) >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2
